questions.text: In a triangle, the segment joining the midpoints of two sides is parallel to which side?

options.a: The side opposite to the vertex

questions.explanation: According to the Mid-point theorem, the line segment joining the midpoints of two sides of a triangle is parallel to the third side.

questions.text: If M and N are midpoints of sides AB and AC of triangle ABC, then the length of MN is:
questions.explanation: By the Mid-point theorem, MN is parallel to BC and MN = 1/2 × BC.

questions.text: The converse of the Mid-point theorem states that if a line segment joining two points divides two sides of a triangle equally, then the segment is:
questions.explanation: The converse states that if a line divides two sides equally, it is parallel to the third side.

questions.text: In the Intercept theorem, if equal intercepts are made on two transversals by a set of parallel lines, then the ratio of the segments on the transversals is:

options.b: Equal to the ratio of the intercepts

questions.explanation: Equal intercepts on transversals imply the ratio of segments on the transversals is 1.

questions.text: If a line segment joining midpoints of two sides of a triangle is 6 cm, then the length of the third side is:
questions.explanation: By Mid-point theorem, segment joining midpoints is half the length of the third side, so third side = 2 × 6 = 12 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are midpoints of AB and AC respectively. If BC = 10 cm, then length of DE is:
questions.explanation: DE = 1/2 × BC = 5 cm by Mid-point theorem.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a condition of the Mid-point theorem?

options.a: Points must be midpoints of two sides
options.b: The segment joining midpoints must be parallel to the third side
options.c: The segment joining midpoints is equal in length to the third side
options.d: The segment joining midpoints is half the length of the third side

questions.explanation: The segment joining midpoints is half the length, not equal, to the third side.

questions.text: If a quadrilateral has its diagonals bisecting each other, then it is a:
questions.explanation: Diagonals bisecting each other is a property of parallelograms.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, if D and E are midpoints of AB and AC, then the triangle formed by points D, E, and B is:
questions.explanation: Triangle DEB is similar to triangle ABC by Mid-point theorem and similarity criteria.

questions.text: Which theorem helps in proving that the segment joining midpoints of two sides of a triangle is parallel to the third side?
questions.explanation: Mid-point theorem directly states this property.

questions.text: If three parallel lines intersect two transversals, then the intercepts on the transversals are:
questions.explanation: According to the Intercept theorem, the intercepts on the transversals are proportional.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, if D and E are midpoints of AB and AC, then the quadrilateral BCED is a:
questions.explanation: By Mid-point theorem, BCED is a parallelogram.

questions.text: The length of the segment joining midpoints of two sides of a triangle is 8 cm. If the third side is x cm, then x equals:
questions.explanation: By Mid-point theorem, segment length = 1/2 × third side, so third side = 2 × 8 = 16 cm.

questions.text: If a line divides two sides of a triangle in the ratio 1:1, then the line is:
questions.explanation: If a line divides two sides equally, it is parallel to the third side (converse of Mid-point theorem).

questions.text: In the Intercept theorem, if three parallel lines intersect two transversals such that the segments on one transversal are 3 cm and 6 cm, then the corresponding segments on the other transversal are:
questions.explanation: Segments on transversals are proportional; if one set is 3 cm and 6 cm, the other set must be in the same ratio, so 3 cm and 6 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are midpoints of AB and AC. If DE = 5 cm and BC = x cm, then x equals:
questions.explanation: DE = 1/2 × BC, so BC = 2 × 5 = 10 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, if a line through midpoint D of AB is parallel to AC and intersects BC at E, then E is the:
questions.explanation: By Mid-point theorem, E is the midpoint of BC.

questions.text: If in triangle ABC, points D and E are midpoints of AB and AC, and F is the midpoint of BC, then quadrilateral DEFB is a:
questions.explanation: Joining midpoints forms a parallelogram inside the triangle.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, if the segment joining midpoints of two sides is parallel to the third side and measures 7 cm, then the perimeter of triangle ABC is at least:
questions.explanation: The segment is half the length of the third side, so the third side is 14 cm; perimeter is at least 14 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, if D and E are midpoints of AB and AC respectively, then which of the following is TRUE?
questions.explanation: According to Mid-point theorem, DE is parallel to BC and half its length.

questions.text: In a triangular park ABC, D and E are mid-points of sides AB and AC respectively. If BC = 80 m, find the length of DE.
questions.explanation: By the Mid-point theorem, DE is parallel to BC and DE = 1/2 × BC = 1/2 × 80 = 40 m.

questions.text: A ladder leans against a wall such that the foot of the ladder is 6 m from the wall. The ladder reaches a window 10 m above the ground. Points A and B are mid-points of the ladder and the wall respectively. Find the distance AB.
questions.explanation: Using Mid-point theorem in triangle formed by ladder and wall, AB joins mid-points of ladder and wall, so AB = 1/2 × length of base (distance from wall) = 1/2 × 10 = 5 m.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, S and T are mid-points of PQ and PR respectively. If ST = 12 cm, find the length of QR.
questions.explanation: By Mid-point theorem, ST is parallel to QR and ST = 1/2 × QR, so QR = 2 × ST = 24 cm.

questions.text: A farmer wants to fence a triangular field ABC. Points D and E are mid-points of AB and AC. If length DE = 30 m, find the length of BC.
questions.explanation: DE is half of BC by Mid-point theorem, so BC = 2 × DE = 60 m.

questions.text: In triangle XYZ, M and N are mid-points of XY and XZ respectively. The length of MN is 15 cm. Find the length of YZ.
questions.explanation: MN = 1/2 × YZ, so YZ = 2 × MN = 30 cm.

questions.text: A triangular plot has sides 50 m, 60 m, and 70 m. Points D and E are mid-points of sides measuring 50 m and 60 m. Find the length of DE.
questions.explanation: DE is parallel to the third side (70 m) and DE = 1/2 × 70 = 35 m. However, since D and E are mid-points of sides 50 m and 60 m, DE is parallel to the third side which is 70 m. So DE = 1/2 × 70 = 35 m.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of sides AB and AC respectively. If the length of DE is 18 m, find the length of BC.
questions.explanation: By Mid-point theorem, DE is half of BC, so BC = 2 × 18 = 36 m.

questions.text: A triangle has sides 24 cm, 32 cm, and 40 cm. Points M and N are mid-points of sides 24 cm and 32 cm. Find the length of MN.
questions.explanation: MN is parallel to the third side (40 cm) and MN = 1/2 × 40 = 20 cm.

questions.text: In triangle DEF, G and H are mid-points of sides DE and DF respectively. If GH = 7 cm, find the length of EF.
questions.explanation: GH is half of EF by Mid-point theorem, so EF = 2 × 7 = 14 cm.

questions.text: In a triangle, the mid-points of two sides are joined to form a segment of length 9 m. If the length of the third side is 20 m, check if the segment length is consistent with the Mid-point theorem.

options.a: Yes, because 9 m is half of 20 m
options.b: No, because 9 m is less than half of 20 m
options.c: Yes, because 9 m is equal to 20 m
options.d: No, because 9 m is more than half of 20 m

questions.explanation: By Mid-point theorem, the segment joining mid-points should be half of the third side, i.e., 10 m. Since 9 m is less than 10 m, it is not consistent.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of AB and AC respectively. A line through E parallel to BC meets the extension of AB at F. If AB = 12 cm and BC = 18 cm, find the length of EF and AF.
questions.explanation: Since E is midpoint of AC, and EF is drawn parallel to BC, by converse of mid-point theorem, F is midpoint of AB extended. EF is equal to BC (since EF || BC and E is midpoint). So EF = 18 cm. Since AB = 12 cm, AF = AB + BF. But F lies on extension of AB, and since E is midpoint of AC, F divides AB extended such that AF = 24 cm.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, M and N are mid-points of PQ and PR respectively. A line through M parallel to QR meets the extension of PR at S such that N lies between P and S. If MN = 5 cm and PS = 15 cm, find the length of QR and NR.
questions.explanation: Since M is midpoint of PQ and MN is parallel to QR, by mid-point theorem MN = 1/2 QR, so QR = 2*5 = 10 cm. N is midpoint of PR, so NR = 1/2 PR. PS = 15 cm and S lies on extension of PR beyond R, so PR = PS - SR. Since N lies between P and S, NR = 7.5 cm (half of PR).

questions.text: In quadrilateral ABCD, E and F are mid-points of sides AB and DC respectively. If EF is parallel to both AD and BC and EF = 6 cm, find the length of AD and BC.
questions.explanation: Since E and F are mid-points of AB and DC respectively and EF is parallel to AD and BC, by mid-point theorem applied to trapezium, EF = 1/2 (AD + BC). Given EF = 6 cm and EF is parallel to AD and BC, so AD = BC = 12 cm.

questions.text: In triangle XYZ, M and N are mid-points of sides XY and XZ respectively. A line through N parallel to YZ meets the extension of XY at P. If MN = 7 cm and XP = 21 cm, find the length of YZ and YP.
questions.explanation: MN is half of YZ (mid-point theorem), so YZ = 2*7 = 14 cm. Since N is midpoint of XZ and NP || YZ, by converse, P divides XY extended such that XP = 21 cm. YP = XY + XP = 7 + 21 = 28 cm.

questions.text: In triangle DEF, G and H are mid-points of sides DE and DF respectively. If GH = 8 cm and the perimeter of triangle DEF is 48 cm, find the length of EF and the perimeter of triangle GHF.

options.a: EF = 16 cm, Perimeter of GHF = 24 cm
options.b: EF = 8 cm, Perimeter of GHF = 16 cm
options.c: EF = 24 cm, Perimeter of GHF = 32 cm
options.d: EF = 16 cm, Perimeter of GHF = 32 cm

questions.explanation: GH is half of EF by mid-point theorem, so EF = 2*8 = 16 cm. Since G and H are mid-points, triangle GHF is similar to DEF with scale factor 1/2, so its perimeter is half of DEF's perimeter = 48/2 = 24 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of sides AB and AC respectively. If DE is extended to F such that EF = DE, find the ratio of the areas of triangle DEF and quadrilateral BCFE, given AB = AC = 10 cm and BC = 12 cm.
questions.explanation: Since D and E are mid-points, DE = 1/2 BC = 6 cm. EF = DE = 6 cm, so F lies on line DE extended such that EF = DE. Triangle DEF is congruent to triangle DFE with half the base and height of ABC, so area(DEF) = 1/4 area(ABC). Quadrilateral BCFE includes triangle BCF and triangle EFC, which together have area 3 times that of DEF, so ratio is 1:3.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of sides AB and AC respectively. If BC = 10 cm, find the length of DE and prove that DE is parallel to BC.
questions.correct_answer: DE = 5 cm; DE is parallel to BC by Mid-point Theorem
accepted_forms: DE = 5 cm; DE is parallel to BC
5 cm; DE || BC
DE equals 5 cm and DE is parallel to BC
questions.explanation: By Mid-point Theorem, the line joining mid-points of two sides is parallel to the third side and half its length. Since D and E are mid-points, DE = 1/2 BC = 5 cm and DE || BC.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, M and N are mid-points of PQ and PR respectively. If MN = 6 cm and QR = 12 cm, verify the Mid-point Theorem and find the length of side QR.
questions.correct_answer: QR = 12 cm; MN = 1/2 QR = 6 cm; MN is parallel to QR
accepted_forms: QR = 12 cm; MN = 6 cm; MN || QR
MN = 6 cm, QR = 12 cm, MN is half of QR and parallel to it
MN equals 6 cm and is parallel to QR which is 12 cm
questions.explanation: Mid-point theorem states MN is parallel to QR and MN = 1/2 QR. Given MN = 6 cm, so QR = 12 cm, verifying the theorem.

questions.text: In triangle XYZ, A and B are mid-points of XY and XZ respectively. If AB = 7 cm and YZ = 14 cm, prove that AB is parallel to YZ and find the length of AB.
questions.correct_answer: AB = 7 cm; AB is parallel to YZ by Mid-point Theorem
accepted_forms: AB = 7 cm; AB || YZ
7 cm; AB is parallel to YZ
AB equals 7 cm and AB is parallel to YZ
questions.explanation: By Mid-point Theorem, AB is parallel to YZ and AB = 1/2 YZ = 7 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of AB and AC respectively. If DE = 8 cm and BC = 16 cm, verify the Mid-point Theorem and find the length of BC.
questions.correct_answer: BC = 16 cm; DE = 1/2 BC = 8 cm; DE is parallel to BC
accepted_forms: BC = 16 cm; DE = 8 cm; DE || BC
DE equals 8 cm and is half of BC which is 16 cm
DE is parallel to BC and DE = 8 cm, BC = 16 cm
questions.explanation: Mid-point Theorem states DE is parallel to BC and DE = 1/2 BC. Given DE = 8 cm, so BC = 16 cm, verifying the theorem.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, M and N are mid-points of PQ and PR respectively. If MN = 5 cm and QR = 10 cm, prove that MN is parallel to QR and find the length of MN.
questions.correct_answer: MN = 5 cm; MN is parallel to QR by Mid-point Theorem
accepted_forms: MN = 5 cm; MN || QR
5 cm; MN is parallel to QR
MN equals 5 cm and MN is parallel to QR
questions.explanation: By Mid-point Theorem, MN is parallel to QR and MN = 1/2 QR = 5 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of AB and AC respectively. If DE = 6 cm and BC = 12 cm, verify the Mid-point Theorem and find the length of DE.
questions.correct_answer: DE = 6 cm; DE is parallel to BC by Mid-point Theorem
accepted_forms: DE = 6 cm; DE || BC
6 cm; DE is parallel to BC
DE equals 6 cm and DE is parallel to BC
questions.explanation: Mid-point Theorem states DE is parallel to BC and DE = 1/2 BC. Given BC = 12 cm, so DE = 6 cm, verifying the theorem.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, M and N are mid-points of PQ and PR respectively. If MN = 4 cm and QR = 8 cm, prove that MN is parallel to QR and find the length of MN.
questions.correct_answer: MN = 4 cm; MN is parallel to QR by Mid-point Theorem
accepted_forms: MN = 4 cm; MN || QR
4 cm; MN is parallel to QR
MN equals 4 cm and MN is parallel to QR
questions.explanation: By Mid-point Theorem, MN is parallel to QR and MN = 1/2 QR = 4 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of AB and AC respectively. If DE = 9 cm and BC = 18 cm, verify the Mid-point Theorem and find the length of DE.
questions.correct_answer: DE = 9 cm; DE is parallel to BC by Mid-point Theorem
accepted_forms: DE = 9 cm; DE || BC
9 cm; DE is parallel to BC
DE equals 9 cm and DE is parallel to BC
questions.explanation: Mid-point Theorem states DE is parallel to BC and DE = 1/2 BC. Given BC = 18 cm, so DE = 9 cm, verifying the theorem.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, M and N are mid-points of PQ and PR respectively. If MN = 3.5 cm and QR = 7 cm, prove that MN is parallel to QR and find the length of MN.
questions.correct_answer: MN = 3.5 cm; MN is parallel to QR by Mid-point Theorem
accepted_forms: MN = 3.5 cm; MN || QR
3.5 cm; MN is parallel to QR
MN equals 3.5 cm and MN is parallel to QR
questions.explanation: By Mid-point Theorem, MN is parallel to QR and MN = 1/2 QR = 3.5 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of AB and AC respectively. If DE = 10 cm and BC = 20 cm, verify the Mid-point Theorem and find the length of DE.
questions.correct_answer: DE = 10 cm; DE is parallel to BC by Mid-point Theorem
accepted_forms: DE = 10 cm; DE || BC
10 cm; DE is parallel to BC
DE equals 10 cm and DE is parallel to BC
questions.explanation: Mid-point Theorem states DE is parallel to BC and DE = 1/2 BC. Given BC = 20 cm, so DE = 10 cm, verifying the theorem.

questions.case_text: In a village, a farmer has a triangular field ABC where AB = 80 m, AC = 60 m, and BC = 100 m. He wants to build a fence along a line joining the midpoints of sides AB and AC to divide the field into two smaller fields. He also wants to know the length of this fence and its orientation relative to side BC.

subparts.text: If M and N are midpoints of AB and AC respectively, what is the length of the fence MN?
subparts.explanation: By Mid-point theorem, MN = 1/2 BC = 1/2 × 80 = 40 m.

subparts.text: What is the orientation of the fence MN with respect to side BC?
subparts.explanation: Mid-point theorem states that the line joining midpoints of two sides is parallel to the third side.

subparts.text: If the farmer wants to find the length of side BC using the fence length MN, what will be the length of BC?
accepted_forms: 80 meters
subparts.explanation: Since MN = 1/2 BC, BC = 2 × MN = 2 × 40 = 80 m.

questions.case_text: A civil engineer is designing a rectangular park PQRS measuring 120 m by 80 m. She wants to place two benches at points M and N, which are midpoints of sides PQ and PS respectively. She needs to find the distance between the benches and verify if the line joining them is parallel to the opposite side QR.

subparts.text: What is the length of the line segment MN joining the midpoints M and N?
subparts.explanation: MN = 1/2 × QR = 1/2 × 80 = 40 m by Mid-point theorem.

subparts.text: Is the line segment MN parallel to the side QR?

options.a: Yes, MN is parallel to QR
options.b: No, MN is perpendicular to QR
options.d: MN is neither parallel nor perpendicular to QR

subparts.explanation: By Mid-point theorem, line joining midpoints of two sides is parallel to the third side.

subparts.text: If the length of MN is 40 m, what is the length of side QR?
accepted_forms: 80 meters
subparts.explanation: Since MN = 1/2 QR, QR = 2 × MN = 2 × 40 = 80 m.
